title: Flutter-only mobile with Rust FFI
scenario: Build an iOS/Android Flutter application where UI is Flutter and all networking, persistence, sync, inference, and agent behavior live in the Rust core behind a Rust FFI boundary.
termination: Stop only when a real simulator or device launch crosses the Flutter/Rust FFI boundary, persists domain state, survives restart, proves provider/domain layering, and passes critic review.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify Flutter beta, Dart codegen, Xcode/Android toolchains, Rust 1.96+, flutter_rust_bridge_codegen version alignment, and device/simulator availability.
prompts.discovery: Read AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, arch standard, Flutter patterns, Rust patterns, FRB configuration, generated file conventions, and existing mobile source layout.
prompts.feynman: Explain Flutter screen→Riverpod provider→domain/data repository→Rust FFI layering and why Flutter must not own networking, persistence, or agent behavior.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess the mobile feature goal, platform targets, device launch proof, restart persistence, offline behavior, parity expectations, and UI/accessibility standard.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze current Flutter features, provider generation, Rust API surface, bridge generation, build scripts, platform linker settings, and persistence flows.
prompts.kbd_spec: Specify domain contracts, Riverpod providers, FRB-generated API, Rust-owned services, UI shell, golden/a11y checks, launch evidence, and restart persistence.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan Rust API changes before Dart adapters, then provider/domain implementation, screen wiring, code generation, platform build repair, launch proof, tests, critic, and retention.
prompts.research: Use official Flutter/Dart/FRB/Rust sources and local templates to confirm current codegen and platform build requirements before implementation.
prompts.implementation: Implement one waypoint at a time. Move domain behavior out of screens, use generated Riverpod providers, regenerate FRB and build_runner outputs, and keep all side effects behind Rust/domain boundaries.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Run code generation, flutter analyze, flutter test, build or launch iOS/Android, create state through the UI, restart, and verify the persisted state returns through the Rust boundary.
prompts.independent_critic: Review layering, generated outputs, platform launch logs, restart persistence, UI parity, and whether any Dart screen directly owns persistence or network behavior.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record toolchain versions, bridge failures, platform fixes, launch evidence, and reusable mobile-scaffold lessons in Karpathy memory.
prompts.recovery: If codegen or launch fails twice for the same reason, stop and return to KBD analysis with exact tool output and platform state.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for missing simulator/device, FFI generation mismatch, platform linker failure, hidden direct persistence in UI, repeated launch failure, or missing restart proof.
